description:
As part of a disaster-recovery drill you, the backup operator, must prove that a restored copy of the DNS/hosts data is usable.  
    A read-only directory already exists that contains the data which would normally be held on the DNS server:

        /home/user/mock_dns/hosts_mapping

    1. What you must do  
       a. Create a new working directory called  
          /home/user/restore_tests  
          (create intermediate directories if they do not yet exist).  

       b. Copy the file /home/user/mock_dns/hosts_mapping into the new directory, keeping the same filename.  
          This copied file represents the “restored” version that you will test.  

       c. Count how many lines (i.e. host records) are present in the copied file.  

       d. For every host record found, look up the IPv4 address that appears on the same line and generate a log entry that proves the hostname-to-address mapping is intact.  
          Use the following exact log-line format (one line per host; do **not** add extra spaces):  

              HOSTNAME|IPv4_ADDRESS|TIMESTAMP_ISO8601_Z  

          • HOSTNAME           – exactly as it appears as the first field in the hosts_mapping file  
          • IPv4_ADDRESS       – exactly as it appears as the second field in the hosts_mapping file  
          • TIMESTAMP_ISO8601_Z– the moment the lookup is performed, written in UTC in the form  
                                 YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ (example: 2025-04-17T09:23:11Z)  

       e. Append one summary line to the very end of the same log file using **this exact format** (again, no extra spaces):  

              TEST COMPLETED: N hosts processed  

          Replace N with the integer you obtained in step (c).  

    2. Where to write the log  
       All log lines (individual host lines + the single summary line) must be written to  

           /home/user/restore_tests/dns_restore.log  

       If the file already exists, truncate it first so the final file contains only the fresh data from this run.

    3. Constraints  
       • You do not have root privileges, so do not modify /etc/hosts or system DNS settings.  
       • Use only the data in /home/user/mock_dns/hosts_mapping for resolutions; external DNS queries are not required.  
       • The entire task can be completed with roughly 5–10 shell commands.  
       • Ensure that the timestamp you record for each host is generated individually (do not reuse the same timestamp for every line).  

    4. Verification expectations (read carefully)  
       After you are done, the automated checker will look for:  
       • The directory /home/user/restore_tests existing.  
       • The file /home/user/restore_tests/hosts_mapping existing and being an exact byte-for-byte copy of the original source file.  
       • The file /home/user/restore_tests/dns_restore.log existing and having:  
         – One line per host record, in the same order as they appear in hosts_mapping,  
           each line following the exact HOSTNAME|IPv4_ADDRESS|TIMESTAMP_ISO8601_Z pattern.  
         – A single summary line at the very end that exactly matches  
           “TEST COMPLETED: N hosts processed” where N is the correct count.  
       • No extra blank lines at the beginning, between entries or at the end of the log file.

    5. Hints (not mandatory but helpful)  
       • `mkdir -p …` will build parent directories as needed.  
       • `cp` is sufficient for copying a single file.  
       • `wc -l` is a quick way to count lines.  
       • `date -u '+%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ'` prints ISO-8601 timestamps in UTC.  
       • Remember to redirect (`>`) or append (`>>`) to files appropriately so that the summary line ends up as the very last line.

    When everything is done correctly the automated tests will confirm the directory exists, the copied file matches, and the log file has the right content and format.
truth:
The initial test image already contains the following file before the agent starts:

        Path: /home/user/mock_dns/hosts_mapping
        Permissions: 0644 (read-only for safety)
        Contents (4 lines, with a single space separating the two fields on each line and a trailing newline at the end of the file):

            backup1.example.com 192.168.10.11
            backup2.example.com 192.168.10.12
            db1.internal.local 10.0.0.21
            db2.internal.local 10.0.0.22

    The automated checker will expect, after successful completion, the following artefacts:

    1. Directory:
        /home/user/restore_tests           (must exist)

    2. File:
        /home/user/restore_tests/hosts_mapping
            – Must be byte-for-byte identical to /home/user/mock_dns/hosts_mapping
            – Permissions may be default (agent is non-root).

    3. Log file:
        /home/user/restore_tests/dns_restore.log
            – Must contain exactly 5 lines.
            – Lines 1-4 must be, in order:

                  backup1.example.com|192.168.10.11|<ISO_TIMESTAMP_1>
                  backup2.example.com|192.168.10.12|<ISO_TIMESTAMP_2>
                  db1.internal.local|10.0.0.21|<ISO_TIMESTAMP_3>
                  db2.internal.local|10.0.0.22|<ISO_TIMESTAMP_4>

              where each <ISO_TIMESTAMP_*>
              • matches the regex  ^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z$
              • Each timestamp value is allowed to differ, but they must be strictly monotonically non-decreasing in the order written (because they are captured sequentially).

            – Line 5 must be:

                  TEST COMPLETED: 4 hosts processed

            – There must be no additional lines (blank or otherwise) before line 1 or after line 5.

    4. File hierarchy / permissions
       The agent need not change any file ownerships or permissions; default user-created modes are acceptable.

    Any deviation from the above – wrong directory path, mismatching copied file, malformed log line, wrong host count, extra blank lines, etc. – will cause the automated grading to fail.
